steps: web identity -> Paperclip PMID lookup (DOI missed) -> Paperclip ls/wc/grep -> local PDF main+S1 slices
notes: Paperclip exposed supplement filenames pbio.2002468.s001.pdf and pbio.2002468.s002.zip; PLOS landing page would expose the same — Paperclip path was a speed win, not a uniqueness win. Reclassified A->B post-adversarial-review per adversarial_review_response.md C4.

steps: web identity -> Paperclip PMID lookup -> Paperclip wc/grep -> supplement check (empty dir + merged content discovered)
conflicts: Paperclip supplements/ empty BUT content.lines contains '# Supplementary Appendix' header
notes: Diagnostic only: Paperclip's grep revealed that the supplement was merged into content.lines without programmatic separator. This is a wrong-content-organization defect to know about, not synthesis value Paperclip added beyond web.

steps: web identity -> Paperclip DOI miss + PMID miss + title search miss -> local PDF (front-matter only) -> fall back to web abstract
conflicts: Paperclip missed entirely (Annual Reviews not indexed)
notes: Paperclip-rescue: web abstract carried the synthesis after Paperclip failed both DOI and PMID lookups.

steps: web identity (final Nat Med) -> Paperclip DOI miss + PMID miss -> Paperclip title search returned medRxiv PREPRINT not final -> fall back to web final abstract
conflicts: Paperclip returned preprint when final was requested
notes: Paperclip-rescue + version-failure: web identified final 20.2% / 3% / 250-day numbers; Paperclip's wrong-version retrieval would have caused KB version pollution.

conflicts: All three arms failed at content level
notes: No added value from any arm beyond identity. Honest E classification — Paperclip miss + OCR failure + pre-1980 NEJM no abstract.

steps: web identity (rate-limited, inline metadata only) -> Paperclip arXiv lookup BOTH SUCCEED -> Paperclip ls/wc inventory -> local PDF (2023 only)
conflicts: arXiv API rate-limited; Paperclip provided primary identity confirmation
notes: Paperclip arXiv lookup succeeded when external arXiv API was rate-limited during this session; backup-when-web-degraded redundancy, not a uniqueness contribution. Reclassified A->F post-adversarial-review per adversarial_review_response.md C4. F added as new classification value.

steps: web identity (v1 2015-04-10 + v2 2015-07-10 + PLOS ONE 2015-08-28) -> Paperclip arXiv lookup -> Paperclip returned version-collapsed record
conflicts: Paperclip collapsed v1/v2/final into single record
notes: Paperclip-rescue/diagnostic mix: web preserved version lineage that Paperclip cannot distinguish. Critical for KB version-aware ingestion.

conflicts: Paperclip returned wrong paper as top hit
notes: Paperclip-rescue: local PDF carried identity confirmation; Paperclip's substitution risk made flagged but explicitly refused.

steps: ClinicalTrials.gov v2 API (registry) -> PubMed esummary + abstract -> Paperclip PMID lookup -> Paperclip search trials/us (false positive NCT04544787 + map UUID error)
conflicts: trial map UUID failure + false-positive NCT04544787
notes: Diagnostic + reading convenience: ClinicalTrials.gov is registry truth. Paperclip's trial-map UUID failure is critical KB knowledge. Paperclip useful for reading paper content but not as bridge engine.

notes: Paperclip-rescue: web abstract carried full synthesis. Paperclip missed despite reasonable target.
